steps.title: Identify the added concern
steps.content: The first sentence gives one reason some justices oppose televising oral arguments: participants might perform for cameras. The second sentence gives another concern: viewers might watch only misleading short clips.

steps.title: Reject contrast and example cues
steps.content: Choice A signals contrast, but the second sentence supports the same position. Choice C signals comparison, which is not the relationship. Choice D signals an example, but the second sentence adds a separate reason.

steps.title: Add a supporting reason
steps.content: Additionally correctly introduces one more reason the justices worry about televising oral arguments. The answer is B.
